coachFindings.actualCopy: ACTIONABLE SIGNAL PRIORITY WATCHLIST Mega Greninja is your priority watchlist. You have enough watchlist evidence to start reviewing the pattern. MISSION MODE Priority watchlist EVIDENCE 5/5 watchlist games SIGNAL STATE Strong enough to review
coachFindings.evidence: Raging Bolt vs Mega Greninja seeded at 9-26-5 across 40 games
coachFindings.action: Keep Mega Greninja on the watchlist and keep tagging what breaks first.
coachFindings.notes: The strongest seeded leak should lead the dashboard.

coachFindings.actualCopy: Opening hands are hurting your results 10
coachFindings.evidence: Dragapult sequencing issue in 13 of 22 losses; Item Lock in 10 of 10 control losses
coachFindings.action: Use the filtered review surfaces to decide what to re-test next.
coachFindings.notes: Review should surface both repeated tag issues and matchup pressure.

coachFindings.actualCopy: Mega Greninja 33% Samples 84 Record 28-45-11 Action Keep logging What to test next 1 Mega Greninja is your priority watchlist. Open review 2 Tag every loss vs Mega Greninja Log a game 3 Compare going first vs second in your recent losses Open review
coachFindings.evidence: Priority leak expected around Mega Greninja at 9-26-5
coachFindings.action: Use the report link and follow-up logging loop from Matchups.
coachFindings.notes: This route should agree with dashboard and review priority language.

coachFindings.actualCopy: v3 Anti-Bench BEST CURRENT READ v2 Vessel Package 47% TC
coachFindings.evidence: Playtest 250 - Raging Bolt Lab | v1 Turbo: 2-10-2, opening 43%; Playtest 250 - Raging Bolt Lab | v2 Vessel Package: 16-15-3, opening 71%; Playtest 250 - Raging Bolt Lab | v3 Anti-Bench: 13-14-5, opening 56%
coachFindings.action: Deck detail should keep highlighting the active experiment and the healthier opener.
coachFindings.notes: Raging Bolt and Gardevoir both have visible version movement.

coachFindings.evidence: Seeded dataset contains exactly 250 matches, so pagination should remain active.
coachFindings.action: Keep logs paginated instead of rendering the full table at once.
coachFindings.notes: Volume should stay usable on desktop and mobile.

Actionable review
TC
COACH SAYS
ACTIONABLE REVIEW
Opening hands are hurting your results

You lose 55% of games when your opening hand is Bad or Okay in your recent games. Compare that to 55 of 148 games with a Good or Great opening hand. Before changing tech cards, check whether this pattern holds across the full sample.

EVIDENCE

56 of 102 games with a bad or okay opening hand were losses.

Log next game
"missed setup" is your most common loss tag

You tagged "missed setup" in 56 of 111 losses with your recent games. It rarely shows up in wins. That is the clearest issue signal in your current data.

INSIGHT

EVIDENCE

56 of 111 losses tagged with "missed setup".

Review losses
Mega Greninja is your priority watchlist

Mega Greninja has your worst win rate in your recent games. Keep logging normally — when this matchup appears, tag what breaks first instead of changing the list based on feel. "missed setup" is the most common loss tag — you tagged it 43 times in those losses.

INSIGHT

EVIDENCE

28-45-11 across 84 games.

View matching games
"key tech mattered" appears often in your wins

You marked "key tech mattered" in 60 of 108 wins with your recent games. That usually signals a tech or line that is worth keeping while you test other changes. Do not cut it before logging more games.

INSIGHT

EVIDENCE

60 of 108 wins tagged with "key tech mattered".

Log next game

desktopRoutes.heading: Profile
desktopRoutes.bodyText:
SixPrizer
Overview
Log game
Review
Logs
Decks
Matchups
Profile
SixPrizer

Community settings

Profile

Update how your SixPrizer identity and testing signal appear to others.

View profile
Refresh public stats
1

SECTION 1

Player identity

Set the public-facing basics of your SixPrizer player identity. Keep it private until you are ready.

Display name
Handle

Lowercase only. Use letters, numbers, underscores, or hyphens. No spaces.

Avatar URL

Optional. Add a clean image URL if you want your public profile to feel more personal.

Country
D
Bio
2

SECTION 2

Competitive profile

Give your profile the context that makes it feel like a serious testing identity, not just an account name.

Favorite archetype
Main deck
Current testing focus

These fields shape your preview and public testing identity, but they do not expose raw decklists or notes.

3

SECTION 3

Privacy and sharing

Decide what other players can discover and what level of testing signal is safe to share.

Profile visibility

Choose how visible your player identity should be.

Private

Only you can view your profile.

Link-only

Anyone with the direct link can view it.

Public

Your profile can be discovered and shared.

Analytics visibility

Public analytics are always summary-first and never raw logs.

Private

Only you can see your testing stats.

Aggregate only

Others see summary trends, never raw logs.

Detailed

Public pages can show the fuller summary model you allow.

FINAL STEP

Save your profile

You can keep everything private until you are ready to share, and public reports stay opt-in.

Saving here updates your identity and sharing controls without exposing raw match details.

Save profile

LIVE PREVIEW

Your SixPrizer player card

This is how your profile will start to feel once you decide to share it.

D
Dom Zimmerman Test

@domz_test

Netherlands

Testing matchup plans, version changes, and tournament prep with SixPrizer.

Private
Private
Raging Bolt
Raging Bolt v3 Anti-Bench
Mega Greninja matchup

PRIVACY SUMMARY

What stays private

Your community profile is controlled, summary-first, and safe by default.

✓

Raw match logs stay private.

✓

Private notes stay private.

✓

Decklists do not become public just because a profile exists.

✓

Public reports are opt-in and summary-only.

✓

Analytics sharing is controlled separately from profile visibility.

Actionable review
TC
COACH SAYS
ACTIONABLE REVIEW
Opening hands are hurting your results

You lose 55% of games when your opening hand is Bad or Okay in your recent games. Compare that to 55 of 148 games with a Good or Great opening hand. Before changing tech cards, check whether this pattern holds across the full sample.

EVIDENCE

56 of 102 games with a bad or okay opening hand were losses.

Log next game
"missed setup" is your most common loss tag

You tagged "missed setup" in 56 of 111 losses with your recent games. It rarely shows up in wins. That is the clearest issue signal in your current data.

INSIGHT

EVIDENCE

56 of 111 losses tagged with "missed setup".

Review losses
Mega Greninja is your priority watchlist

Mega Greninja has your worst win rate in your recent games. Keep logging normally — when this matchup appears, tag what breaks first instead of changing the list based on feel. "missed setup" is the most common loss tag — you tagged it 43 times in those losses.

INSIGHT

EVIDENCE

28-45-11 across 84 games.

View matching games
"key tech mattered" appears often in your wins

You marked "key tech mattered" in 60 of 108 wins with your recent games. That usually signals a tech or line that is worth keeping while you test other changes. Do not cut it before logging more games.

INSIGHT

EVIDENCE

60 of 108 wins tagged with "key tech mattered".

mobileRoutes.heading: Profile
mobileRoutes.bodyText:
SixPrizer

Community settings

Profile

Update how your SixPrizer identity and testing signal appear to others.

Home
Log
Review
Logs
Decks
Match
Profile
View profile
Refresh public stats
1

SECTION 1

Player identity

Set the public-facing basics of your SixPrizer player identity. Keep it private until you are ready.

Display name
Handle

Lowercase only. Use letters, numbers, underscores, or hyphens. No spaces.

Avatar URL

Optional. Add a clean image URL if you want your public profile to feel more personal.

Country
D
Bio
2

SECTION 2

Competitive profile

Give your profile the context that makes it feel like a serious testing identity, not just an account name.

Favorite archetype
Main deck
Current testing focus

These fields shape your preview and public testing identity, but they do not expose raw decklists or notes.

3

SECTION 3

Privacy and sharing

Decide what other players can discover and what level of testing signal is safe to share.

Profile visibility

Choose how visible your player identity should be.

Private

Only you can view your profile.

Link-only

Anyone with the direct link can view it.

Public

Your profile can be discovered and shared.

Analytics visibility

Public analytics are always summary-first and never raw logs.

Private

Only you can see your testing stats.

Aggregate only

Others see summary trends, never raw logs.

Detailed

Public pages can show the fuller summary model you allow.

FINAL STEP

Save your profile

You can keep everything private until you are ready to share, and public reports stay opt-in.

Saving here updates your identity and sharing controls without exposing raw match details.

Save profile

LIVE PREVIEW

Your SixPrizer player card

This is how your profile will start to feel once you decide to share it.

PRIVACY SUMMARY

What stays private

Your community profile is controlled, summary-first, and safe by default.

✓

Raw match logs stay private.

✓

Private notes stay private.

✓

Decklists do not become public just because a profile exists.

✓

Public reports are opt-in and summary-only.

✓

Analytics sharing is controlled separately from profile visibility.
